attention3.ptxt: Check the following conditions before parking brake inspection and adjustment.

item.ptxt: No air is trapped in brake system.
Rear brake shoes or brake pad are not worn beyond limit.

attention3.ptxt: Brake warning light should be checked when ignition is “ON”.

item.ptxt: With parking brake lever pulled up, inspect the following items. If check result is not OK, adjust or replace parking brake cable.
Operation of parking brake lever is smooth.
Operate parking brake lever with force of 200 N (20.4 kgf, 45.0 lbf) as shown, counting number of clicks ratchet notches generate.

attention3.ptxt: One click sound corresponds to one notch.

sdm1.sdm-condition: When lever pulled up at 200 N (20.4 kgf, 45.0 lbf)

item.ptxt: Release parking brake lever and make sure brake warning light turns off. Then check that brake warning light turns on within 1 notch when parking brake lever is pulled up. If check result is not OK, check and, if necessary, repair the following items.

attention3.ptxt: Check that ABS warning light is turned off.

item.ptxt: Brake fluid switch (attached to brake master cylinder reservoir)

s1.ptxt: Loosen adjusting nut (1) until parking brake cable becomes free of tension.
Start engine and depress brake pedal more than 15 times with about 150 N (15.3 kgf, 33.7 lbf) so as to obtain proper drum-to-shoe clearance.
Stop engine and adjust parking brake lever stroke to specified value with adjusting nut (1).

attention3.ptxt: Parking brake lever must be released while adjusting.

s1.ptxt: Check equalizer (2) inclination angle. If angle is not as specified, inspect parking brake cable and rear brake shoe linings inclination.
After completion of adjustment, make sure that brake drum is not dragging.
Loosen adjusting nut until parking brake cable becomes free of tension.
Start engine and depress brake pedal more than 3 times with about 150 N (15.3 kgf, 33.7 lbf).
Stop engine and make sure that lever (1) of rear brake caliper contacts the pin (2). If the lever does not contact the pin, repeat steps 3) and 4) again.
Tighten adjusting nut (1) so that parking brake drags when parking brake is pulled by one notch.
Confirm that parking brake lever stroke is between 4 and 9 notches when the parking brake lever is pulled with 200 N (20.4 kgf, 45.0 lbf).
Check equalizer (2) inclination angle. If angle is not as specified, inspect parking brake cable, rear brake caliper and rear brake pads.
Release parking brake and make sure that parking brake is not dragging.
